meta.title: TRITON - Schneider Electric Analysis and Disclosure
meta.description:
This presentation on the TRITON malware was groundbreaking. Not in the technical details, although those were important, but in the fact that an ICS vendor analyzed an attack on their equipment and disclosed what the attack did to their system. They set the bar for other vendors to meet or exceed.

It gets serious at 7:20. Schneider Electric actually fixed the bugs in the malware and ran it in their lab to see exactly what it did to the targeted version of the Tricon Controller. "The intent was not to crash these controllers but to have power over them".

There are a lot of interesting technical details in this including how the malware would hide once installed. And they released tools to detect it and remove it from memory.
